title: Director of AI Creative Systems
description: CSC Generation is the AI-native holding company re-engineering omnichannel retail. We acquire iconic brands and transform them with Genesis, our operating platform combining a Data Fabric, Automation Engine, proprietary tools, and shared services to modernize operations, elevate customer experience, and expand margins. With $1B+ in revenue across 13 brands, our portfolio includes Sur La Table, Backcountry, One Kings Lane, and others that serve as real-world innovation labs. Reports to: Chief Growth Officer Location: Austin, TX (Remote); Toronto, ON (Remote); All other US & Canada (Remote) About The Role We are building a new creative production and testing capability from scratch — one that uses AI-native workflows to generate photoreal imagery, video, and copy at speed, then connects that output to structured testing loops across paid media, email, and site. This function does not exist yet, and the person in this role will define and build it. As Director of AI Creative Systems , you will architect the end-to-end system that moves creative from brief to prompt to asset to launch, establish the quality bar, and personally ship the first generation of production-grade work. Success in the first year means creative output that is materially faster and cheaper without looking cheap, repeatable testing systems that increase learning velocity, and stronger integration between creative generation and commercial decision-making across our portfolio of brands. This is a lean team. You will own a lot, move fast, and make decisions with full end-to-end responsibility. What You'll Do Build the Creative Production System Design and implement AI-native workflows for image, video, copy, resizing, versioning, QA, approvals, and deployment. Build the full pipeline from brief to prompt to asset to review to launch — starting with your own hands before scaling through others. Establish standards for prompts, references, model selection, asset naming, storage, metadata, and quality control. Create reusable templates, playbooks, and automations so brands can scale output without rebuilding the system every time. Train internal teams to operate within those systems rather than relying on one-off heroics. Generate High-Fidelity Creative Produce and direct AI-generated creative that meets brand standards and holds up next to traditionally produced work. Push for photoreal, commercially usable output rather than obvious AI-looking artifacts. Develop workflows for product imagery, concepting, lifestyle scenes, motion, and derivative asset generation across channels. Make deliberate decisions about when to use AI-only workflows, hybrid human-plus-AI workflows, or traditional production based on quality and brand risk. Automate Creative Testing Build systems that generate creative variants for testing across paid social, display, email, landing pages, and other growth channels. Partner with performance marketing and growth teams to connect creative concepts to test design, campaign structure, naming, routing, and readouts. Increase testing throughput without destroying learning quality. Develop segmentation-aware creative workflows that tailor concepts, formats, hooks, or offers to different audiences while staying within legal, platform, and brand guardrails. Help the organization move from subjective creative debates to structured experimentation and measurable creative learning. Operate as a Cross-Functional Builder Work closely with growth, performance marketing, ecommerce, merchandising, brand, and leadership teams to identify and remove creative bottlenecks slowing growth. Translate emerging tools into practical operating leverage instead of trend-chasing. Stay hands-on enough to ship the first wins yourself while building infrastructure others can use. Act as both function owner and dangerous builder: set the bar, build the system, and prove it in live work. Required Qualifications 7+ years of experience spanning creative production, performance
score_reason: Perfect fit: senior director building AI creative systems from scratch, 100% remote with explicit Canada/US flexibility, USD-based, aligns precisely with Travis's national CD background and deep hands-on AI expertise.

Operate as an anchor leader within the division, sitting across multiple opportunities/clients and guiding the pod’s strategic-to-creative output. Use performance data and post-campaign insights to refine creative strategy frameworks, ensuring learnings translate into stronger future concepts and platform approaches Provide direct leadership, including improving output quality, sharpening strategic rigor, and raising the standard of social-first thinking. Serve as the primary point of alignment for senior creative partners for cohesion, quality control, and strategic consistency. Shape and strengthen strategic narratives, pitches, and creative storytelling for new business and client engagements. Support and, when required, lead client presentations, translating strategy into clear creative direction and actionable next steps. Advise on creative direction during content development/production, ensuring alignment with platform trends and performance insights. Develop and evolve repeatable creative strategy frameworks (platform playbooks, concepting structures, signal-to-idea methodologies) that elevate consistency and quality across teams. Translate cultural trends, platform updates, and performance insights into actionable creative guidance for clients and internal teams. Contribute hands-on to ideation and concept development when needed to unblock teams or elevate creative thinking. Partner with cross-functional teams (Business Development, Client Services, Media, Measurement) to ensure strategy and creative recommendations convert in

100 or more new ad creatives per week across UGC, static, video sales letter, mashup, talking-head, and lifestyle formats Review daily performance data, including hook rate, hold rate, click-through rate, conversion rate, cost per acquisition, and return on ad spend, and translate those signals into the next production sprint Retire creatives that fail to perform, double down on those that do, Background in subscription DTC beauty, skincare, or health supplements Comfort working across multiple direct response formats at high cadence Close prior collaboration with a paid social media buyer title: Creative Strategist

Be part of a major retail expansion : A significant retail partnership is launching in weeks. The creative work you do directly shapes how new games scale on DTC and feed into retail. A clear growth path to Creative Director : As the team under you grows, the role grows with it. You're already at the top of the creative function on day one. Compensation that reflects the importance of the seat : Strong base plus a performance bonus structure that rewards finding winners. title: Responsable, image de marque
description: À propos de Mila Fondé par le professeur Yoshua Bengio de l'Université de Montréal, Mila rassemble des chercheurs spécialisés en intelligence artificielle et plus précisément en apprentissage automatique, apprentissage profond et apprentissage par renforcement. Reconnu mondialement pour ses importantes contributions au domaine de l'apprentissage profond, Mila s'est particulièrement distingué dans la modélisation du langage, la traduction automatique, la reconnaissance d'objets et les modèles génératifs. Depuis 2017, Mila est le fruit d'une collaboration entre l'Université de Montréal et l'Université McGill, en lien étroit avec Polytechnique Montréal et HEC Montréal. Mila s'est donné pour mission d'être un pôle mondial d'avancées scientifiques qui inspire l'innovation et l'essor de l'intelligence artificielle (IA) au bénéfice de tous. Pour en connaitre davantage, veuillez consulter https://mila.quebec/ Sommaire du poste Mila invite un·e Responsable du Design de Marque à rejoindre son équipe pour faire rayonner l'identité visuelle de notre organisation. Au sein d'un collectif dynamique et en plein essor, vous jouerez un rôle clé pour incarner et faire évoluer l'image de Mila, référence mondiale en IA. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next. About Xd Netflix Experience Design (XD) is a multidisciplinary team of designers, content strategists, motion and audio designers, design engineers, and program managers—unified around a single mission: Entertaining The World. We partner closely with Product, Engineering, Consumer Insights, Data Science, and User Research to create experiences that make members happy and measurably move the business forward. We're a lean, matrixed organization that pushes the Netflix experience beyond expectation. We operate on a foundation of farming for dissent, content not control, and informed captains—prioritizing collaboration and principled decision-making over hierarchy. About Member Xd This leader will be responsible for leading the core member experience worldwide and creating personalized entertainment experiences that help members discover and connect with the content they love. We do this by defining the app architecture and foundational user experience strategic frameworks, ensuring excellence and platform innovation across TV, Mobile, and Web experiences, prioritizing personalized content discovery, leveraging human-centered AI, and ensuring each member’s experience on Netflix reflects what they truly value. About You You'll lead a lean remote design organization of seasoned designers, while partnering closely with cross-functional leaders across the business. We are looking for a senior executive who prides themselves on being able to strategically execute, is not motivated by title but inspired by impact, and is skilled at inspiring senior, high-caliber talent to do their best work. You'll drive creative strategies that challenge the status quo and push user experience forward through emerging technologies—often in spaces where design hasn't yet established a foothold or set the rules. Comfort with ambiguity and uncharted territory is essential. We're looking for someone who can operate effectively in a highly matrixed organization, build and strengthen horizontal cross-functional partnerships, and influence the creative thinking that keeps Netflix at the forefront of global entertainment. Responsibilities Shaping strategic direction for developing new and existing member models, including the following and more: Discovery & Browsing – Shape home, rows, carousels, search, genre hubs, collections, and personalization UI to help members efficiently find something great to watch. Playback & Viewing – Craft intuitive, high‑quality viewing experiences across devices, including player controls, subtitles/audio, previews, interactive formats, and kids viewing modes. Kids & Family – Build kid‑appropriate, family‑friendly experiences with tailored navigation, parental controls, and shared viewing flows. Globalization & Accessibility – Ensure experiences are localized, culturally relevant, and accessible (assistive tech, visual contrast, cognitive load) across regions and device ecosystems. AI - Establish Member XD as an AI-forward experience design organization. Operationalizing AI across every craft to drive creative excellence, accelerate innovation and artifact creation, and deliver adaptive, intelligent experiences Partnering closely with the VP of Design, VP of Product, VP of Engineering, and Design Directors across XD to align on priorities and a strategic direction for the growth of Member XD. Facilitating cross‑functional strategic decisions with the ability to stress‑test executive assumptions and plans respectfully, using data and scenarios to improve the quality of decisions. Creating an environment that fosters high-quality, metrics-driven experimentation
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at scale with global scope and cutting-edge tech, but design-focused UX role lacks traditional advertising/brand campaign DNA that defines Travis's track record.

Team and Organizational Development: Structure and scale the team for both deep execution and long-horizon bets. Cette personne sera aussi à l'aise de présenter la vision d'un plan directeur à un promoteur de complexe hôtelier que de coacher un designer junior. Notre équipe excelle dans la réalisation de projets de jeux d'eau et de projets de moyenne envergure, mais elle est prête à évoluer vers des mandats plus ambitieux. Les architectes paysagistes et firmes d'aménagement , où l'intégration au site et la collaboration sont au cœur des projets. Les clients commerciaux (complexes hôteliers, parcs d'attractions et établissements d'accueil), où l'expérience immersive, la narration visuelle et la planification directrice créent la valeur. Pour soutenir cette croissance, nous recherchons un leader capable de structurer les meilleures pratiques, d'élever la qualité du design et de faire progresser l'ensemble de l'équipe. Ce que vous ferez Développer notre approche en design Assurer la direction créative et le mentorat d'une équipe de designers de projets (gestion fonctionnelle). Développer une méthodologie de conception adaptée à nos différents types de projets. Mettre en place des approches reproductibles en expérience utilisateur, narration spatiale et planification directrice. Créer des standards de design, une bibliothèque de références visuelles et des modèles de présentation. Réaliser des projets d'envergure Diriger la conception de projets commerciaux et de grande envergure, particulièrement dans les secteurs hôteliers, des centres de villégiature et des parcs d'attractions. Concevoir des présentations qui mettent en valeur la vision du projet et l'expérience utilisateur. Collaborer avec les équipes de gestion de projets et d'opérations afin de préserver l'intention de conception jusqu'à la réalisation. Contribuer à l'innovation Identifier des occasions de développement de produits à partir des projets, des visites de sites et des échanges avec les clients. Réaliser des entrevues auprès des clients et des utilisateurs afin d'identifier les besoins émergents. Collaborer avec les équipes Produits et Commerciales pour transformer ces apprentissages en nouvelles opportunités. Ce que nous recherchons Plus de 8 ans d'expérience en design, dont au moins 3 ans dans un rôle de leadership ou de direction créative. Expérience démontrée dans des projets axés sur l'expérience utilisateur ou le design expérientiel (environnements thématiques, complexes hôteliers, espaces publics, hospitalité ou projets similaires). Maîtrise de 3ds Max, SolidWorks, Unreal ou d'outils équivalents de visualisation 3D. Expérience au sein d'équipes multidisciplinaires réunissant ingénieurs, gestionnaires de projets et partenaires de fabrication ou de construction. Diplôme universitaire en architecture de paysage, architecture, design d'intérieur, design industriel, design de l'environnement ou domaine connexe. Un titre professionnel (AAPQ, OAQ, APDIQ ou équivalent) constitue un atout. Excellentes aptitudes en communication et en présentation en français et en anglais. (Un niveau avancé d'anglais est requis.) Excellente capacité à raconter une histoire convaincante en transformant des concepts techniques en expériences inspirantes. Pourquoi joindre Vortex? Chez Vortex, l'innovation, la collaboration

title: Head of Marketing
description: We are hiring a Head of Marketing to own the growth and brand engine of AMMD across every channel. You will own the full funnel — brand, paid media, lifecycle, content, eCommerce, and the data that ties them together — and you will be accountable for blended CAC, LTV, contribution margin, and the growth rate of the business. Above all, this role is the steward of a physician-led brand. AMMD exists because of Dr. Myers’ clinical authority — her medical credentials, her bestselling books, and the protocols she has built over years of patient care. Your job is to amplify that authority, not dilute it: to put Dr. Myers — the celebrity physician at the heart of the brand — at the center of how we acquire, convert, and retain, and to ensure every claim and every campaign earns the trust her name carries. You will partner with her directly and protect the clinical credibility that is the brand’s single greatest growth asset. The right person is equal parts strategist and operator: comfortable setting a 12-month growth strategy and quarterly plan from a blank page, but also close enough to the work to read a media buy, pressure-test a CRO roadmap, sharpen a campaign brief, and call the next bet. title: Head of Brand
description: About Primer Primer is a growing network of K–8 schools restoring the timeless foundations of American education. Inspired by the original teaching primers that shaped a nation of readers, thinkers, and builders, our schools begin with what has always worked, and build for what lies ahead. Close-knit learning environments. Skilled educators. A culture that takes kids seriously as learners and leaders. Primers shaped the first 200 years of American children. Primer will ready them for the next. About The Role Primer has all the makings of an iconic brand: a big vision to solve one of the hardest problems in America, real stories of lives we’ve changed across the country, and strong cultural tailwinds pushing us forward. We hear from parents, educators, legislators, and community leaders every day about the lives we’re changing — from families who felt stuck in the traditional system and now see their child thriving at Primer, to parents who “feel like they have their child back” after a few months at Primer. Your job is to harness this momentum and craft a brand that millions of families from all walks of life trust with the most important decision they make each year: their child’s school. You will own the entire surface area of Primer’s brand — from our big-picture narrative to individual brand assets across visual design, photography, film content, and copy. You’ll inherit our existing brand systems, which you see today across Primer.com, and be tasked with growing this foundation into the future of Primer’s brand. There Are Three Unique Challenges In This Role First, our brand must be both aspirational and accessible. Most education brands opt for pure exclusivity or pure accessibility, but that won’t work for Primer. Second, we are a central marketing & brand org with dozens (and soon hundreds) of local Primers. These campuses are the primary way families experience the Primer brand post-enrollment, and nailing the local manifestation of our brand will require systems that scale down to (and empower) those working at the campus level. Finally, you’ll need to develop (or have) a nuanced intuition for how Primer should fit into the overall education ecosystem. Education is tough, riddled with many strong opinions, which presents both a challenge and an opportunity. If you shape our public perception with conviction — and succeed — you will have crafted one of the all-time great American brands. Responsibilities Define what Primer stands for. Own our narrative — where we fit in the broader education ecosystem, how we show up, what we're for and what we're against. Craft our brand norms. These are the standards that define our voice, language, and visual identity. The kind of thing a campus team or a writer can actually pick up and use, not a deck that sits in a drive. Build the local engine. We have hundreds (and soon thousands) of local educators who are the face of Primer to our families. Creating a consistent brand experience is a unique challenge, and you will own it end-to-end. Consistency without bottlenecks. Build the operating system that dials in every public-facing artifact we ship. Fight the approval-chain-by-vibes with clear standards and lightweight checks against them. Partner with Growth, Admissions, and Product to establish the messaging, identity, and creative standards used across marketing, admissions, and the product experience, enabling each team to execute within those standards. Lay the groundwork for employer brand and PR, so our story lands with parents, future staff, and the public alike. What we're looking for A strong operator who loves building systems. You're clear-eyed about the complexity of a central brand function with dozens of local spokes. Someone who's done a lot with a little. You've held a high bar in a resource-constrained environment. A brand leader who’s done it before — you’ve built brands that feel both aspirational and accessible, and understand what it takes to connect with every
score_reason: Senior brand leadership with strong creative scope, but no salary listed, no remote confirmation, and education sector (not advertising/marketing core) creates notable gaps.

Founded in 2014 by our CEO Chelsea Hirschhorn, Frida was built to make the raw reality of parenting a little easier to navigate (and a lot less overwhelming). It all started with one legendary snot-sucker — the NoseFrida — and has grown into a 200+ product lineup that supports families through every stage: from fertility and postpartum recovery to baby care and beyond. We don't shy away from the stuff no one else wants to talk about — nipple pain, diaper blowouts, or the emotional rollercoaster that comes with keeping a tiny human alive. We call it like it is, solve the problems that actually matter, and build products that help parents feel seen, supported, and totally capable. You can now find Frida products in 50+ countries and thousands of stores across the U.S., from the biggest national retailers to your neighborhood grocery aisle. Under Hirschhorn's leadership, Frida has become a category leader by challenging taboos, championing honesty, and supporting families at every stage of parenthood and beyond, earning acclaim on TIME's 100 Most Influential Companies, TIME Best Inventions, Fast Company Most Innovative Companies and Fast Company Brands That Matter. But the real win? Knowing we're helping parents everywhere feel a little more human and a little less alone. How You Will Make an Impact Reporting to the SVP, Brand Management, the VP, Brand Management will lead our commercial initiatives for Frida brands and be responsible for managing and growing brands by defining strategy and driving growth initiatives across the existing and new lines of products. You are the technical and creative anchor of the org — the team gets sharper around you, and when you're in the C-suite room, customers stop second-guess ing the timeline. We are not pre-sales. Pair with a P40 in the morning and pitch a Fortune 100 CMO in the afternoon, and both walk away sharper. Demo your own work — script, produce, edit. The demos you create become the org's reference demos. What You'll Bring The bar — what we filter hardest for: You've earned adoption where it's hardest. Not one win — a track record of walking into the most resistant users, the most fractured stacks, and the highest-stakes renewals, and driving repeat, at-scale adoption anyway. You measure yourself in renewals saved and churns averted, not features shipped. You set direction others follow. You've set technical or creative direction in a prior role — not just executed someone else's spec. Point to the "before me / after me" change you drove, and to the people who got better because of you. C-suite credibility. You hold the room with CMOs, CIOs, and CTOs and walk out with the deal or the renewal — translating tech to business value without losing the engineers in the room. The floor — we don't teach this: Creative content supply-chain craft at architect depth. Foster a culture of creative excellence, curiosity, and relentless iteration. Champion "Siblings, Not Twins": Drive a visual system that provides shared DNA while enabling bespoke, expressive executions across every touchpoint — digital, OOH, film, experiential events, thought leadership, board documents, in-product, and more. We don't do "one-size-fits-all." Every expression of Atlassian should feel personal and essential. Ship, Don't Deck: Oversee art executions from concept to launch with uncompromising attention to typography, photography, motion, and detail. Help drive and deliver prototypes for a “reps” based creative model. Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Partner with Brand, PMM, Events, Demand, Comms, OCMO, OCEO, Product Design, Sales, and Eng to deliver a unified brand experience. You'll help shape what the creative department becomes. title: Chief Marketing Officer

This role oversees brand, digital, communications, product marketing, market intelligence, and customer insights, and partners closely with executive leadership to align marketing priorities with the organization’s strategic and financial objectives. Essential Functions Develop and lead the enterprise marketing strategy in alignment with the organization’s mission, business plan, and growth objectives. Establish and advance brand strategy, market positioning, and messaging to strengthen awareness, differentiation, and reputation. Oversee integrated marketing programs across digital, content, communications, public relations, social media, events, and other relevant channels. Direct demand generation and customer acquisition strategies that improve pipeline performance, conversion, retention, and revenue contribution. Lead market research, competitive intelligence, and customer insight efforts to inform strategic decisions, product positioning, and campaign priorities. Partner with executive leadership, sales, product, operations, and finance to align marketing initiatives with enterprise priorities and measurable business outcomes. Define key performance indicators for the marketing function and use analytics, attribution, and reporting to evaluate effectiveness and optimize investment. Develop and manage the marketing budget, vendor relationships, and resource allocation to ensure operational efficiency and return on investment. Build, lead, and develop a high-performing marketing organization that fosters accountability, innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Ensure marketing practices comply with applicable laws, regulations, and brand standards while protecting the organization’s reputation. Required Competencies Strategic marketing leadership with the ability to translate business priorities into integrated go-to-market plans and measurable outcomes. Expertise in brand management, demand generation, digital marketing, communications, customer segmentation, and market positioning. Strong business and financial acumen, including budget management, forecasting, performance measurement, and marketing ROI analysis. Advanced analytical capability with experience using data, customer insights, and market intelligence to guide decision-making. Executive-level communication skills, including the ability to influence senior stakeholders, present strategy, and align cross-functional teams. Deep understanding of evolving marketing technologies, automation platforms, CRM ecosystems, and data-informed campaign management. Demonstrated success leading organizational change, scaling marketing operations, and building high-performing teams. Strong project and operational management skills with the ability to prioritize initiatives and execute in a fast-paced environment. Exercises sound judgment, professionalism, and discretion in handling sensitive and high-impact matters. Demonstrates strategic mindset, curiosity, and learning agility in response to market shifts and organizational change. Builds trust and credibility through transparent communication, integrity, and follow-through. Leads with accountability and resilience while maintaining focus on enterprise priorities and results. Fosters collaboration and inclusion across functions, encouraging diverse perspectives and shared ownership. Drives innovation by challenging assumptions, encouraging experimentation, and translating ideas into action. Develops talent through coaching, feedback, and a commitment to team growth and succession readiness. Maintains a customer-focused orientation and balances brand stewardship with commercial performance. Education and Experience Bachelor’s degre
score_reason: Senior CMO title fits, but no mention of remote work, no salary transparency, and debt resolution/B2B services lacks the creative campaign scope Travis built at T&Pm and tms.

title: Vice President Marketing
description: Pivotal Partners is exclusively partnered with a leading Agentic AI platform, redefining enterprise AI adoption by enabling full AI nativity. This is to be the first marketing hire with incredible opportunity to build out the function, strategy and processes as well as hire a team of industry leading experts. The Role As VP of Marketing, you’ll build and scale the marketing function from the ground up. This is a hands-on, strategic role for a proven marketing leader who thrives in early-stage, high-growth environments. You’ll drive end-to-end marketing efforts — from demand generation and performance marketing to content and sales enablement — all tightly aligned with GTM and revenue outcomes. You’ll work closely with the executive team to define positioning, drive pipeline, and build the brand as a category-defining player in the Agentic AI and automation space. What You’ll Own: Build the marketing function from scratch , including team structure, tooling, and strategy. Lead GTM alignment across marketing, sales, and product — with a focus on measurable ROI. Own growth marketing strategy — including performance marketing, ABM, and demand generation. Oversee digital marketing , SEO/SEM, and paid media channels , ensuring scalable, data-driven execution. Create and manage content strategy — including technical whitepapers, sales decks, case studies, and tailored collateral for multiple personas. Run A/B tests and experiments to optimize messaging, campaigns, and conversion paths. Drive sales enablement by equipping the revenue team with effective content, playbooks, and tools. Own and optimize the marketing budget , allocating spend for impact and efficiency. Build and lead a high-performing marketing team as we scale. What We’re Looking For: Proven experience building and leading a marketing function at an early-stage B2B tech company (Series A–C ideally). Strong understanding of the AI, data, and automation ecosystem. Deep expertise across key marketing pillars: growth/performance marketing, demand gen, ABM, content, digital, SEO, and paid channels . Track record of driving revenue growth and pipeline in close collaboration with Sales. Strategic mindset with strong analytical and executional ability . Excellent communicator with the ability to create compelling narratives across technical and business audiences. Experience managing budgets, vendors, and cross-functional initiatives. A player-coach mentality — willing to roll up sleeves in a fast-moving environment.
score_reason: Strong senior scope and AI-native positioning align with Travis's hands-on AI expertise, but VP Marketing (vs. Creative/Brand) and missing remote/location clarity create notable gaps.

title: VP Marketing
description: About the Client: Imagine a world where AI predicts maritime disruptions before they happen, orchestrating thousands of vessels, ports, and supply chains in perfect harmony while protecting national security and saving lives. This is the frontier where artificial intelligence meets defence intelligence and the ancient art of navigation. The Opportunity: The company is shifting from technology-led to sales-led, and marketing is the next function to build out properly. A fractional CMO has carried it so far and will overlap briefly with the new hire before transitioning out. The VP Marketing owns the end-to-end strategy and execution across both motions: positioning, ICPs and messaging per segment, the demand and pipeline engine, sales enablement, the event-heavy defence calendar, content, and the brand and website refresh tied to the rebrand. This is a player-coach leading a small team (a returning marketing generalist and an open content marketing role). Just as important: this person sits at the leadership table with the credibility to say what marketing is and own that narrative, because parts of the leadership team have never worked with marketing in a startup before. They need someone who can hold that room. The Skills/Requirement Needed to Succeed: Domain experience in maritime (ocean and shipping, not land logistics) or defence and security. One of the two is required; both is a unicorn. A track record of scaling a B2B SaaS: $5M to $50M+, or $50 > $100M. Large enterprise, corporate company marketers are not the right profile. A hands on player-coach who will get scrappy and into the weeds while setting strategy. If you need a team of 10 this role is not a match. Strong product marketing: positioning, segmentation, persona development, competitive differentiation, proof-point storytelling. Operational discipline with marketing metrics: funnel definitions, conversion, attribution basics, pipeline reporting, and working closely with Salesforce and RevOps. Executive presence and credibility with a CEO, COO, and technical leadership. An A-player who can go toe to toe with the leadership team and own the marketing narrative. Martyn Bassett Associates: Martyn Bassett Associates is a team of recruiters solving the ambitious goals of startups and scale-ups by recruiting world-class sales, marketing, and product management talent who deliver growth and scale. We’ve helped over 1500 candidates land their next big opportunity at companies like Shopify, Extreme Networks, Wattpad, Loopio, Readdle, ClearCo, Workleap, Invicti, Resolve Pay, The Herjavec Group, GE Energy, GE Health, Enable, RBC Ventures, Unibuddy, Cognota, Thalmic Labs (now Google Glass), Eloqua (now Oracle Marketing Cloud) to name a few.
score_reason: Senior marketing leadership with creative scope and remote potential, but maritime/defence domain expertise is a hard requirement Travis lacks, and no salary/location clarity given.

Deep expertise in demand generation, revenue marketing, product marketing, digital marketing, marketing operations, and customer marketing. Demonstrated ability to build pipelines, improve lead quality, increase funnel conversion, and drive measurable contribution to ARR growth. Experience leading marketing across a multi-product portfolio, multiple customer segments, and complex buyer journeys. Passionate about incorporating AI in

title: Design Technologist, Agentic AI & Marketing Automation

This role will prototype AI-driven automation experiences, build functional integrations between workflow management systems, agentic AI tooling, and 3P platforms to enable our marketing team to scale communications production across email, portal notifications, and website channels with greater speed, quality, and efficiency. You will serve as the technical and UX bridge between marketing stakeholders and AI engineering teams, turning ambiguous automation concepts into tangible, interactive experiences that teams can evaluate, test with users, and use to move automation projects forward. This role requires hands-on technical execution and technical leadership, you'll implement AI technologies, provide technical integration support, create reusable prototyping frameworks, and develop reference implementations that raise the bar for how the team builds and delivers AI-powered solutions. You will design, execute, and troubleshoot AI-driven solutions, set prototyping standards for the team, and influence UX and technical approaches across adjacent teams to drive operational efficiency at scale. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role. The ideal candidate brings strong prototyping skills, experience with the UX/UI behind marketing workflows, deep comfort working across web technologies and emerging AI platforms, and the ability to creatively solve difficult business and technology constraints. You will also cross-share learnings with global teams to drive best practices and insights. Key job responsibilities Design and implement agentic AI workflows for channel production automation using Amazon AI tools and external platforms Transform UX and workflow concepts into working code (Python, JavaScript, Node, AWS, API integrations). Prototype and validate agentic use cases from concept to pilot, contributing directly to productivity goals. Work backward from the customer experience; collaborate with product, design, and engineering to iterate on optimal experiences and identify UX/workflow issues before dev/test when fixes are less costly Evaluate 1P and 3P AI tools for integration with core marketing systems, accounting for real-world constraints. Develop reusable prototyping methods, agent frameworks, and reference implementations Provide hands-on technical integration support, debugging workflows and implementing solutions to unblock team initiatives Define and optimize performance metrics for AI-driven workflows, measuring impact on conversion, retention, and operational efficiency Share frameworks, patterns, and learnings with global marketing teams to scale AI automation best practices Basic Qualifications 6+ years of front-end technologist, engineer, or UX prototyper experience Have an available online portfolio Have coding samples in front end programming languages Experience developing visually polished, engaging, and highly fluid UX prototypes Experience collaborating with UX, Product, and technical partners Experience in one or more of the following: Native mobile development (Android/iOS), Python, Objective-C, Swift, C#, HTML5/CSS3/JavaScript, web and server programming and Responsive/Adaptive design, Java, SQL Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of databases and AWS database services: ElasticSearch, Redshift, DynamoDB Experience with machine learning (ML) tools and methods Experience designing and developing IT or technical training materials and content 4+ years of workflow experience Experienc

title: AI Creative Lead
description: AI Creative Lead Key Responsibilities The Dell Creative Studio team produces breakthrough creative work at global scale — for one of the world’s most recognized brands — and does it by putting technology at the center of how that work gets made. The AI Creative Lead operates like an art director with the fluency of a builder: leading creative concept development while using AI to move from idea to finished deliverable across more directions, and at more speed, than was previously possible. This role suits someone genuinely curious — energized by tools that didn’t exist last year, restless with “how we’ve always done it,” and as comfortable chasing a craft detail as chasing a new technique. You’re opinionated about the work but open-minded about the methods, and you’d rather figure out what’s newly possible than wait to be told. Lead concept development across projects, setting creative direction and bringing a strong, differentiated point of view. Shape how the organization thinks about AI in creative work — bringing clear vision and deep command of where and how it adds value, where craft still leads, and how the two combine. Pioneer new creative approaches that AI makes possible — unlocking ideas, formats, and executions that weren’t feasible before. Work fluidly at every scale of the work — from complete assets to individual creative fragments and components — creating, modifying, iterating, and finishing as the project demands, to a polished professional standard. Stay ahead of a fast-moving landscape — exploring new AI tools as they emerge and setting the standard for how they’re used to make exceptional creative work. Partner with business stakeholders to translate their goals — and the need for speed — into creative output, using AI to get there. Steward quality, brand consistency, and the rights/ethical considerations that come with AI-generated content. Maintain creative momentum, anticipating and resolving production issues before they escalate. Essential Requirements Strong creative sensibility with a proven ability to evaluate, direct, and elevate creative work. Ability to produce high-quality content quickly using AI, with the skill to get the best results from available models, even when preferred tools or access are restricted. Deep, current fluency with generative AI tools across your medium — plus the craft to take their output the last mile: catching where AI falls short, and refining imagery, video, and voiceover so the final work feels intentional and unmistakably human. Hands-on experience with leading AI models and creative tools — including Adobe, Google, Jasper, Claude, and Synthesia — and the adaptability to master new ones as the landscape shifts. A track record of pushing creative methods forward — developing new approaches, not just applying existing ones. An experimental mindset that runs ahead of the curve — you don’t just adopt new tools quickly, you probe what they make newly possible, and you aren’t attached to any single one. Exceptional communication skills — verbal, written, and presentation — including the ability to bring others along and influence without authority. A portfolio demonstrating both creative judgment and command of AI tools, ideally with visible process rather than only polished end results. Preferred Qualifications Experience owning creative concept development on significant projects or campaigns — agency, studio, or in-house. A track record of inventing new creative formats or approaches with AI — work that others go on to build from. Fluency across more than one creative medium (e.g., still, motion, design, copy), or the range to direct across them. Some familiarity with the rights, licensing, and provenance landscape around AI-generated content.
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at global scale with deep AI integration—perfectly aligned with Travis's CD background and hands-on AI expertise; remote work not explicitly stated but tech company norm; missing salary/location specifics but no disqualifying restrictions.

title: Director of Exhibition Design and Production
description: Position Summary Reporting to the Deputy Director and Chief Experience Officer, the Director of Exhibition Design and Production leads the experiential design, production, and installation of the museum’s diverse and innovative exhibitions, permanent collection displays, and other visitor experiences with Asian art and cultures. Leading the design process from concept to installation and creating designs as well as directing other designers and producers, the Director works closely with curators and other collaborators to create inspirational experiences with Asian art and cultures that align with the museum’s mission, vision, values, and goals, and that are technically feasible, sustainable, and affordable. Essential Functions Design and Production Working closely with curators, artists, and other creative and organizational collaborators, conceives and develops and/or oversees conception and development of original experience designs, including the visitor journey, gallery architecture, layout of objects, exhibition furniture, display cases, and media environments, for temporary exhibitions and other projects. Devises creative solutions for the responsible display of art and creates exhibitions that consider and engage the visitor. Balances creative excellence with conservation requirements, visitor accessibility, budget constraints, and technical feasibility. Reviews design proposals with Conservation and Registration to ensure the safety of the art, and with Engineering, Security, Facilities, Guest Experience, Public Programs, Special Events, and other departments as needed to ensure clear communication and efficiency between exhibitions and visitor, building, and event needs. Integrates labels, signage, and interactive media with overall experience design and the visitor journey, working with internal or external editors, graphic designers, and digital content creators as needed. Participates in the assessment, design, and implementation of design projects for the museum that are not related to exhibitions and projects, working with Engineering, Facilities, and other departments as required. Leads the design process through construction, overseeing the production of detailed construction drawings and other fabrication documents based on final approved designs and developing production, installation, and related staffing plans for each exhibition and project. Directs the Production and Preparation team, as well as contractors, in fabricating and installing exhibitions to the highest professional standards. Troubleshoots any issues that arise between design and fabrication or with building and installation on the floor. Ensures that all safety standards and training requirements are met for all Exhibition Design and Production staff and contractors, and in compliance with OSHA. Troubleshoots design, fabrication, and installation issues as needed during an exhibition and on an ongoing basis for permanent collection displays and other permanent or semi-permanent visitor experiences. Produces presentation materials for senior staff, as needed. Archives final “as-built” drawings, installation views, and any other relevant materials. Team Leadership and Operations Management Provides leadership, management, and mentoring support to team members to maintain a highly skilled and motivated team. Stays current with design innovations, emerging technologies, and best practices in museum exhibition design. Collaborates with stakeholders to create culturally responsive exhibitions that honor the art and cultures represented. Ensures exhibitions are ADA compliant. Champions sustainable and environmentally responsible design practices in material selection and fabrication methods. title: Forward Deployed Creative [US]
description: About Luma Luma’s mission is to build unified general intelligence that can generate, understand, and operate in the physical world. So, we are working on training and scaling up multimodal foundation models for systems that can see and understand, show and explain, and eventually interact with our world to effect change. Where You Come In This is a rare opportunity to define a new category at the intersection of creativity, sales, and education. As our foundational Forward Deployed Creative, you will be the catalyst that transforms our technological marvel into the indispensable tool for the world's leading creators. You won't just be running a playbook; you'll be leveraging your deep expertise to write it in real-time, pioneering the workflows that will define the future of AI-powered storytelling. Architect and prototype novel, AI-accelerated production workflows, helping our partners move faster and level up their storytelling. Create hands-on, high-craft content using Dream Machine, setting the bar for what's possible and producing inspirational case studies. Consult with partner leadership, translating complex technical capabilities into tangible creative and business opportunities. Collaborate internally as an on-the-ground extension of our Product, Partnership, and Sales teams, bringing real-world insights back to shape our roadmap. Build the foundation for the Forward Deployed Creative function by setting the tone and creating the playbook for partner engagement. Educate and inspire the creative community, acting as a key evangelist for Luma and the new medium of generative video. Who You Are Significant professional experience in a role like Creative Technologist, Technical Director, or a similar client-facing, senior creative position at a top-tier agency, studio, or tech company. An outstanding and non-negotiable portfolio of shipped, high-craft creative work that demonstrates exceptional taste, technical skill or storytelling ability. A "builder" and "pioneer" with a proven history of creating new things from scratch in ambiguous, fast-paced environments. Deep expertise in creative production pipelines (VFX, animation, motion graphics, or production) and the ability to design and implement new workflows. Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to act as a strategic consultant to senior creative and business leaders. A genuine passion for the creative potential of AI and our mission to expand human imagination. What Sets You Apart (Bonus Points) Hands-on experience with generative AI creative tools (e.g., Midjourney, Runway, Pika, Stable Diffusion). A public profile as a thought leader or creator in the emerging tech or generative art space. Experience at an innovative creative agency (e.g., Buck, The Mill, Media.Monks) or an in-house tech/brand lab (e.g., Google Creative Lab, Apple Marcom). Compensation The base pay range for this role is $150,000 – $200,000 per year. To go beyond language models and build more aware, capable and useful systems, the next step function change will come from vision. Renowned for revolutionizing digital content creation, Beast Industries encompasses a diverse portfolio of ventures that extend far beyond its origins on YouTube. With a mission to entertain, inspire, and create significant social impact, Beast Industries operates across various domains including digital media, philanthropy, consumer products, and innovative business initiatives. At Beast Industries, we believe in the transformative power of digital media and its potential to entertain, educate, and effect positive change. Our commitment to innovation, creativity, and philanthropy drives us to explore new frontiers, create unforgettable experiences, and build a legacy that inspires future generations. Turn early-stage ideas into structured, scalable creative programs Role Overview This role exists to translate high-level creative ideas into structured initiatives that can be executed across the organization. The Director of Creative Development partners closely with the Head of Development to shape new programs, partnerships, and narrative platforms from concept through development. You will operate at the intersection of creativity, strategy, and execution. The focus is not on producing individual campaigns, but on building the frameworks, direction, and alignment that allow multiple teams to execute effectively. The Role You will own the development and structuring of creative initiatives across brand, content, partnerships, product, and marketing. This includes defining how ideas take shape, aligning stakeholders, and driving initiatives forward from early concept to executable program. This role requires strong creative judgment combined with operational thinking. You will work in an environment where ideas evolve quickly and require structure, clarity, and momentum to become real. What You’ll Do Translate high-level ideas into structured initiatives, programs, and partnership concepts Develop frameworks, concept documents, and development roadmaps Package initiatives into clear narratives for internal alignment and decision-making Partner with cross-functional teams to gather input and shape cohesive programs Align stakeholders across brand, content, product, partnerships, and marketing Define development pathways from early concept through execution readiness Identify internal and external partners required to bring initiatives to life Drive progress and momentum across initiatives with minimal oversight Support incubation of new brand platforms, partnerships, and content programs Expand early ideas into scalable, repeatable creative programs What You’ll Bring 8–12+ years of experience in creative development, brand innovation, strategic partnerships, or related fields Experience structuring multi-team creative initiatives or programs Strong ability to translate ambiguous ideas into clear frameworks and plans Proven cross-functional collaboration across creative, product, and business teams Ability to operate in environments where ideas evolve and direction is fluid Strong strategic thinking and storytelling skills Benefits The Perks, Why Work On the MrBeast Team We are redefining what entertainment and storytelling look like at global scale. Every piece of content we publish reaches millions and influences culture in real time. This is your opportunity to lead the team that decides how those moments come to life across every screen. Competitive Salary Generous Medical (Blue Cross Blue Shield), Dental, Vision and company-paid Life Insurance Company contributions to employee Health Savings Accounts (HSA) 401k Plan with Safe Harbor company-matching Flexible vacation policy and paid company holidays Company-provided technology package Relocation assistance where applicable, including travel and company-provided housing for the first 90 days
score_reason: Senior creative leadership in entertainment/media with scaling framework expertise, but lacks traditional advertising/brand DNA and salary/location details are missing.

Now we're looking for a marketing leader who can tell our story, amplify our brand, and build the growth engine for our next chapter. We're looking for a Vice President – Marketing to lead that journey. You'll work closely with the founder and executive leadership to shape our go-to-market strategy, build and scale a world-class marketing organization, strengthen our brand, and drive predictable revenue growth. If you're energized by building from the ground up, solving complex growth challenges, and creating lasting business impact, we'd love to meet you. What You'll Own Shape and execute the global marketing strategy aligned with business growth and revenue goals. Build, mentor, and scale high-performing teams across demand generation, product marketing, content, brand, digital marketing, and communications. Own demand generation and pipeline growth through integrated inbound, outbound-assisted, ABM, partner, digital, and field marketing programs. Partner closely with Sales to sharpen our ICP, messaging, pipeline quality, forecasting, and revenue outcomes. Craft our product narrative through positioning, launches, customer storytelling, sales enablement, and competitive intelligence. Build a data-driven marketing organization with clear KPIs, attribution, analytics, and scalable marketing operations. Lead the marketing tech stack, budgets, agencies, and operational excellence while fostering a culture of experimentation and continuous improvement. What We're Looking For 15+ years of marketing leadership experience in a B2B SaaS company. Proven success building and scaling marketing teams in a fast-paced startup environment. Strong demand generation background with a track record of building predictable pipeline and driving revenue growth. Experience leading end-to-end marketing across demand generation, product marketing, brand, content, and digital marketing. Deep understanding of enterprise B2B buying journeys and complex sales cycles. Experience leading, managing, or collaborating with high-performing marketing teams based in India, with the ability to build and scale distributed organizations. Strong expertise in marketing technology, CRM, marketing automation, analytics, and attribution. A strategic, data-driven leader who's equally comfortable thinking big and rolling up their sleeves. Experience in Enterprise AI, Intelligent Document Processing (IDP), Document Automation, or Enterprise SaaS is a strong plus. Why Join Us Build and shape the marketing function at a defining stage of the company's growth. Partner directly with the founders and influence company strategy. Solve meaningful go-to-market challenges in one of the fastest-growing AI categories. Build a team, create a brand, and leave a lasting mark on the business. Competitive compensation, meaningful ownership, and the opportunity to help build something extraordinary. Think you're the right fit? Look forward to hearing from you.

They blend cutting-edge tech with data-driven insights to unlock every student's full potential. They're on a mission to disrupt the broken education system and unleash the true potential of gifted minds. Forget the one-size-fits-all approach that leaves brilliant students bored and disengaged. You will own the modern tea brand platform — positioning, voice, campaign system, creative standards, PR, organic social, and creator strategy — and run the integrated marketing calendar that keeps Brand, Growth, and Retail in rhythm. Through product development you will also serve as marketing’s launch PM, coordinating briefs, packaging inputs, claims, and creative routing until each item hands off to Retail & Shopper for commercialization. Organic Social & Creative Pipeline: Own day-to-day organic social and the brand and campaign creative pipeline — briefing and shipping work with internal and external partners, and holding the standard even where production runs through partners. Creator / UGC Strategy: Build the creator engine and content standards, partnering with Growth so creator and PR assets are whitelisting-ready for paid social, TikTok Shop, and retail media. PR & Brand Narrative: Manage earned media, founder and brand storytelling, and the narrative that supports retailer and Series B moments. Brand Health: Track awareness, message recall, sentiment, and perception — the leading indicators that show the brand is gaining traction. Qualifications Experience: 6–8+ years in brand, content, creative strategy, or integrated marketing — ideally CPG/beverage or a high-growth consumer brand. Proven Track Record: Built or meaningfully evolved a brand platform, campaign system, or creative engine from a low base; scaling a brand through a Series B or comparable inflection is a strong plus. Craft: Strong writing, taste, and strategic judgment; can turn positioning into briefs, packaging direction, content, and retailer-facing storytelling. Operating Discipline: Launch and project-management rigor — calendars, owners, status, approvals, and cross-functional follow-up — plus experience managing creative, PR, creator, production, or social partners without overcomplicating the process. title: Head of Growth & Marketing
description: BFF Robots is not a robotics company in the traditional sense. We are a platform. Anyone a consumer, a creator, a business, a healthcare operator can go to bffrobots.com, upload a photo or type a description, and we manufacture a custom humanoid robot built around their vision. Starting at $2,509. On top of the hardware, we are launching Genesis Humanoid Control an AI operating system that gives every BFF Robot a persistent identity, memory, and voice. Your robot remembers you. It grows with you. It becomes yours. We are at the beginning of something massive. And we need a Head of Growth & Marketing who can build the engine that takes us from early momentum to household name. What You Will Own Consumer Growth (B2C): Turn the BFF Robots creator experience into a viral loop. Every person who designs a custom robot on our platform is a story worth telling. You will build the content strategy, creator partnerships, and social presence that makes BFF Robots the most talked-about brand in robotics across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and beyond. Creator & Influencer Ecosystem: Identify, recruit, and build long-term relationships with creators, makers, futurists, and tech enthusiasts who will become BFF Robots ambassadors. This is not influencer marketing as usual these are co-creators who will design robots, document the experience, and bring their audiences into the BFF Robots universe. B2B & Enterprise Pipeline: Partner directly with the founder to build awareness and inbound demand among healthcare operators, retail chains, hospitality groups, and government agencies. You will develop the case studies, thought leadership content, and direct outreach campaigns that open doors at the C-suite level. Brand & Community: Own the BFF Robots brand voice across every channel. Build the community of robot builders, Genesis users, and EIR partners that makes BFF Robots more than a product — a movement. Revenue: This role is tied directly to sales. You will own the top of the funnel and be accountable for qualified leads, conversion rates, and revenue attribution across all three segments. What We Are Looking For 3–7 years of experience in growth marketing, brand marketing, or content strategy — ideally at a consumer tech, hardware, or high-growth startup A proven track record of building audiences from scratch and converting them into customers Deep fluency in short-form video, creator culture, and social platform dynamics. You know what makes something spread and go viral Experience with B2B marketing and enterprise content is a strong plus Data-driven: you track what works, cut what does not, and optimize relentlessly A genuine passion for robotics, AI, or emerging technology. You need to believe in what you are selling Self-directed and entrepreneurial: this is a high-autonomy role at an early-stage company; you will set the strategy and execute it What You Get A front-row seat at a company building something genuinely new the first consumer humanoid robot platform with AI identity and persistent memory Direct access to the founder and full creative latitude to build the marketing function from the ground up Competitive base compensation + performance bonuses tied to revenue milestones Equity participation — you are building this with us, not just for us Remote-first with flexibility The chance to tell a story that has never been told before This is not a job for someone who wants to manage a content calendar. This is a job for someone who wants to build a brand that changes how the world thinks about robots.

THE ROLE We're looking for a Director of Marketing (or Director of Demand Gen, depending on your background) to join our growing team at a pivotal moment in Tapcart's growth. You'll be a key partner to the Head of Marketing, owning our demand generation engine while also contributing to broader marketing strategy, GTM planning, and team leadership. How You Will Make An Impact Own and scale our demand generation engine: You'll take full ownership of multi-channel pipeline generation — paid media, email, events, content, partner channels, SEO/AEO — with accountability for qualified pipeline and ROI. Bring strategic marketing leadership to the team: You'll help define Tapcart's marketing strategy at the category level — refining our ICP targeting, messaging, campaign architecture, and go-to-market approach. You'll be someone the Head of Marketing can think out loud with and hand the keys to (and vice versa). Build and optimize GTM systems: You'll architect and maintain the systems that power our go-to-market motion: HubSpot infrastructure, lead scoring, attribution models, nurture workflows, and signal-to-sequence programs. You build things that work while you sleep. Use AI to 10x our output: You'll be at the forefront of AI-driven marketing, deploying tools and automations that multiply the team's output — from trigger-based outbound and content systems to workflow automation and performance analysis. You build with AI, not just use it. Cross-functional collaboration and leadership: You’ll driving alignment with Sales, Rev Ops, Product, and CS and contribute to how we structure and prioritize as the teams grow. You're a player-coach who makes the people around them better. Connect brand to pipeline: You'll ensure Tapcart's campaigns and content aren't just producing impressions, they're producing pipeline. You'll collaborate on brand positioning and campaign concepts while holding yourself accountable to business outcomes. What Skills You'll Need Proven demand generation leadership: You've owned multi-channel demand programs in a B2B SaaS environment and have a track record of building qualified pipeline. You've managed real budgets and can speak the language of marketing and sales metrics/KPIs. Strategic marketing instincts: You can zoom out and develop a positioning framework or GTM strategy, then zoom in and execute a campaign. You're not purely a performance marketer — you understand brand, narrative, and how they compound with paid and organic channels. AI-powered operational leverage: You use AI tools to build systems and workflows — automating research, enrichment, content production, and reporting — not just to write copy faster. You can evaluate new tools quickly and deploy them with purpose. Deep marketing tech fluency: You have strong working knowledge of HubSpot (or a comparable MAP) and can

title: Creative Director
description: Creative Director Set the vision for how we look, sound, and feel across books, campaigns, and brand. Brave Books publishes children's books and adult nonfiction books. We create subscription services. We run paid ad campaigns. We manage YouTuber relationships where the book is often their first brand extension. Right now, creative direction is distributed — different people making decisions in different directions. We need someone who owns the aesthetic and makes sure every piece of the company reflects a coherent vision. What you'll actually do: World Building. You're the keeper of narrative worlds across our publishing portfolio. For established worlds like Freedom Island, you maintain character bibles, world rules, visual language, and narrative consistency. For new worlds, you help conceptualize them from scratch — understanding the mythology, tone, character archetypes, and rules that make a world feel cohesive and real. You work with writers, illustrators, and product teams to ensure every book extends or reinforces the world it inhabits. Whether it's a YouTuber's first book that launches a new world or the 40th book in an existing universe, you make sure readers feel like they're stepping into something intentional and interconnected, not a random collection of stories. Storytelling Lead. You understand story structure, character development, and narrative arc. For both children's and adult nonfiction books, you'll work with our writers and AI tools to ensure every book has a compelling narrative throughline. For children's books, you'll make sure the story serves the values we're trying to teach. For adult nonfiction, you'll make sure the narrative hooks readers and creates resonance, not just information dumping. You'll give story feedback, shape manuscripts before they're "done," and know when something is working and when it's falling flat. Campaign visual direction. Every launch has a landing page, email sequences, ad creative, and social assets. You'll set the visual direction for each campaign — color palette, photography style, typography — and make sure the execution matches the vision. Brand evolution. Our brand is currently "values-driven children's publisher + adult nonfiction partner." As we scale, are we leaning into that? Are we creating sub-brands? You'll help us think through who we are and how we look. Creator collaboration. When we partner with a YouTuber, they have a visual world — their content style, their audience aesthetic. You'll help our teams understand that world and build book designs that feel authentically theirs, not "made by the publisher." Hire and direct creative freelancers. You'll work with illustrators, designers, photographers, and production vendors. You'll brief them, review their work, give feedback that makes it better, and know when to push and when to step back. Build creative guidelines. Our designers shouldn't have to ask you about every decision. You'll create a shared language — typography standards, color palettes, layout principles — that lets the team make good decisions independently. What we're looking for: A point of view. You have taste. You can articulate why one design works and another doesn't — not "I like this" but "This version creates confidence, clarity, and trust. That one creates confusion." You can defend decisions with reasoning. Appreciation for craft but impatience with vanity. You respect good design, but you know it serves a business function. A beautiful book that doesn't sell is still a failure. Collaboration skills that actually work. You can take feedback without becoming defensive. You can give feedback without crushing people. You can argue for a design direction and then let go when the business case says otherwise. Fluency across mediums. You understand what works on a bookshelf, on an email screen, in an Instagram story, and on an Amazon listing. Different contexts require different approaches. Bonus: Publishing design experience. Children
score_reason: Senior creative leadership with strong integrated scope (brand, narrative, multi-channel), but no explicit remote confirmation, no salary stated, and publishing/books focus is slightly left of Travis's advertising/campaign DNA.
